name: matrAIx Persona Dimension Space
note: Flat unified dimension set. Every sampled persona is assigned exactly one value per dimension. The 'category' tag is for UI grouping/filtering only — it does not nest the schema. The full reachable persona space is the product of all value counts; the 8.3B headline is the corpus of behaviors already simulated within it.

dimensions.label: Region
dimensions.description: World region the persona is based in.

dimensions.label: Urbanicity
dimensions.description: Settlement density of where they live.

dimensions.label: English proficiency
dimensions.description: Command of English (the eval lingua franca).

dimensions.label: Domain
dimensions.description: Primary field of work or study.

dimensions.label: Highest education
dimensions.description: Highest level of formal education completed.

dimensions.label: Emotional state
dimensions.description: Mood at the moment of interaction.

dimensions.label: Intent
dimensions.description: What they want from the agent.

dimensions.label: Expertise gap
dimensions.description: Their expertise relative to the task.

dimensions.label: Trust level
dimensions.description: How much they trust the agent.

dimensions.label: WildChat_State
dimensions.description: U.S. state where user accessed WildChat (geographic dimension from IP geolocation).

source_origin.note: Privacy-first design: WildChat intentionally excludes traditional demographics (age, gender, education, occupation). Only geographic dimensions available.

dimensions.label: WildChat_Country
dimensions.description: Country where user accessed WildChat (geographic dimension from IP geolocation).

source_origin.note: Privacy-first design: WildChat intentionally excludes traditional demographics (age, gender, education, occupation). Only geographic dimensions available.

dimensions.label: WildChat_HashedIP
dimensions.description: Privacy-preserving hashed IP address from WildChat (technical/geographic dimension).

source_origin.note: Privacy-first design: WildChat intentionally excludes traditional demographics (age, gender, education, occupation). Only geographic dimensions available.

dimensions.label: Code Comment Style
dimensions.description: Preference for inline comments, documentation blocks, or minimal commenting in code.

dimensions.label: Code Summary/TLDR Documentation
dimensions.description: Whether developer includes a summary, TLDR, or executive overview at the beginning of files or functions.
values: Includes TLDR for complex functions only

dimensions.label: Code Naming Verbosity
dimensions.description: Style of naming variables, functions, and classes - verbose descriptive vs. terse abbreviated.

dimensions.label: Indentation and Whitespace Style
dimensions.description: Preference for indentation width, tabs vs spaces, and whitespace formatting.

dimensions.label: Code Structure Preference
dimensions.description: Preference for functional, object-oriented, procedural, or imperative programming paradigm.

dimensions.label: Function/Method Length Preference
dimensions.description: Preference for small focused functions vs larger multi-purpose functions.

dimensions.label: Error Handling Style
dimensions.description: Approach to error handling - try/catch blocks, explicit returns, graceful degradation, or silent failures.

dimensions.label: Abstraction and Generalization
dimensions.description: Tendency to create reusable abstractions and generic solutions vs solving specific problems directly.

dimensions.label: Dependencies and Library Usage
dimensions.description: Preference for using libraries and frameworks vs writing custom implementations.

dimensions.label: Performance vs Readability Priority
dimensions.description: Whether developer prioritizes code performance optimization or code readability and maintainability.

dimensions.label: Testing and Code Coverage Approach
dimensions.description: Stance on unit tests, integration tests, TDD, and test coverage requirements.
values: Mix of unit and integration tests

dimensions.label: Refactoring Frequency and Cleanliness
dimensions.description: How often code is refactored, cleaned up, and how much tolerance for technical debt.

reference_sources.note: 6 domains + 25 facets. HEXACO personality trait grounding. Register domains/facets as constructs; do not copy full scale items without checking official terms.
reference_sources.reason_added: Adds 6 major personality domains (Honesty-Humility, Emotionality, Extraversion, Agreeableness, Conscientiousness, Openness) plus 25 facet-level constructs for fine-grained personality grounding beyond Big Five.

reference_sources.note: 70 Big Five facet labels. Fine-grained personality trait grounding.
reference_sources.reason_added: Provides 70 validated Big Five facet labels for detailed personality trait coverage and assessment of specific behavioral tendencies within each domain.

reference_sources.note: 276 scale labels; 3319 item pool entries. Public-domain trait construct and item reference.
reference_sources.reason_added: Offers 276 public-domain trait construct labels and 3319 item pool entries for construct-level grounding and measurement validation of personality dimensions.

reference_sources.license: arxiv paper/reference; verify project, code, dataset, and generated taxonomy license before reuse
reference_sources.note: 12 top-level categories; long-tail persona attribute taxonomy. Candidate attribute coverage and subcategory grounding; requires review before becoming dimensions.
reference_sources.intended_use: Candidate attribute coverage and subcategory grounding
reference_sources.reason_added: Contributes 12 top-level persona categories and long-tail attribute taxonomy for comprehensive candidate attribute coverage and subcategory organization.

reference_sources.note: 135 protocol fields. Sociopsychological persona protocol reference.
reference_sources.reason_added: Adds 135 sociopsychological protocol fields grounding persona schema in tested simulation logic and identity/values/beliefs frameworks.

reference_sources.note: 6518 variables. Official social survey grounding for attitudes, trust, religion, politics, work, family, behavior, and life context.
reference_sources.reason_added: Provides 6518 official social survey variables for grounding in population-level attitudes, trust, religion, politics, work, family, and behavioral data.

reference_sources.note: 153 questionnaire items. Values, beliefs, trust, politics, religion, and social attitudes grounding.
reference_sources.intended_use: Values, beliefs, trust, politics, religion, and social attitudes
reference_sources.reason_added: Adds 153 cross-national survey items for values, political beliefs, religious attitudes, trust, and social worldview dimensions across diverse populations.

reference_sources.note: Curated demographic and population variables. Official demographic grounding layer.
reference_sources.reason_added: Grounds 135 curated demographic and population variables in official U.S. Census data for validated demographic, education, employment, household, and language dimensions.

reference_sources.note: 3 theoretical levels: dispositional traits, characteristic adaptations, narrative identity. Schema theory and narrative identity grounding.
reference_sources.intended_use: Schema theory and narrative identity grounding
reference_sources.reason_added: Provides theoretical foundation (dispositional traits, characteristic adaptations, narrative identity) for schema architecture and multi-level persona construction.

reference_sources.note: World-belief constructs including safe, enticing, alive and associated facets. Worldview grounding.
reference_sources.reason_added: Adds 26 worldview constructs (safe, enticing, alive and associated facets) for belief-based persona differentiation beyond traditional political/religious dimensions.

reference_sources.note: Behavioral inhibition and activation constructs. Motivation and affective disposition grounding.
reference_sources.reason_added: Contributes 4 behavioral inhibition/activation constructs for motivation and affective disposition grounding in neurobehavioral approach systems.

reference_sources.note: Perseverance and long-term effort constructs. Motivation and persistence grounding.
reference_sources.reason_added: Adds 2 perseverance-of-effort and consistency-of-interests constructs for long-term motivation and persistence behavior grounding.

reference_sources.note: Malleability-of-ability belief construct. Cognitive and motivation grounding.
reference_sources.reason_added: Adds 1 malleability-of-ability belief dimension for cognitive motivation and learning orientation differences across personas.
